claims.claim_text: The AI Act requires providers and deployers of AI systems to ensure a sufficient level of AI literacy among relevant staff and people acting on their behalf.
claims.notes: The Commission states that Article 4 applies to providers and deployers and requires measures to ensure sufficient AI literacy.

claims.claim_text: The AI literacy obligation becomes a compliance concern by August 2026.
claims.notes: The obligation already applies from 2 February 2025; supervision and enforcement by national market surveillance authorities start in August 2026. The card compresses that timeline into one deadline.
claims.next_action: re-search and verify with stricter source tier rules

claims.claim_text: Luxembourg employer training co-funding via INFPC / Guichet exists and is relevant to training products.
claims.notes: Guichet states that private-sector businesses in Luxembourg can apply for co-funding of continuing vocational training, with exclusions for mandatory regulated-profession training.

claims.claim_text: Luxinnovation Fit 4 AI / AI support exists as a public AI support route.
claims.notes: Luxinnovation publicly offers Fit 4 AI support. The reviewed public material does not prove that this exact workshop format is directly eligible on the card's terms.

claims.claim_text: 14.4% of Luxembourg enterprises used AI technologies in 2023.
claims.notes: Eurostat reports Luxembourg as one of the highest adopters in the EU at 14.4% for 2023.

claims.claim_text: Only provider combining EU AI Act compliance + hands-on practice + audit documentation.
claims.notes: House of Training already offers Luxembourg AI training that combines AI Act framing, practical AI use and data-protection/risk content, so the exclusivity claim is too strong.
claims.next_action: falsify fully and rewrite or drop

claims.claim_text: The AI Literacy Certificate has external compliance significance.
claims.notes: No public source reviewed shows that a provider-issued certificate from this workshop has official regulatory standing.
claims.next_action: treat as open question, not as fact

claims.claim_text: Audit-ready documentation from this workshop is sufficient for compliance use.
claims.notes: No public source reviewed confirms regulator or authority acceptance of this product's documentation.
claims.next_action: treat as open question, not as fact

claims.notes: No primary public source confirming this exact figure was found in the review.
claims.next_action: treat as open question, not as fact

claims.notes: The reviewed sources do not confirm this amount as it is used in the card's compliance framing.
claims.next_action: treat as open question, not as fact

claims.claim_text: SME Package AI 70% (up to €17,500) applies to this workshop.
claims.notes: Public AI support exists, but the workshop-level eligibility claim is not directly confirmed by the reviewed sources.
claims.next_action: treat as open question, not as fact
